_README: === FIELD DEFINITIONS — HOT-RELOADABLE CONFIGURATION ===
This file defines every data field that appears on a worklist study/exam.
The system re-reads this file every 30 seconds, so changes take effect on
the NEXT batch of newly created studies (already-created studies are not affected).
=== HOW TO ADD A NEW FIELD ===
1. Copy any existing field entry from the 'fields' array below.
2. Change the 'name' (internal key), 'display_name' (human-readable label), and 'description'.
3. Set 'required' to true if every study MUST have this field, or false if it's optional.
4. Choose a 'generation_strategy' from the list below and fill in its parameters.
5. Save this file. The system picks up the new field within 30 seconds.
=== GENERATION STRATEGIES EXPLAINED ===
--- random_from_pool ---
What it does: Picks one random value from a list stored in a separate JSON file.
When to use:  When you have a fixed set of values to choose from (e.g., patient names).
Parameters:   'pool_file' = name of the JSON file inside data/pools/ folder.
'pool_key'  = the key inside that JSON file that holds the list.
Example:      A field that picks a random patient name from 1000 stored names.
--- random_from_pool_keyed ---
What it does: Picks a random value from a list, but which list depends on ANOTHER field's value.
When to use:  When the choices depend on context. E.g., study descriptions differ by modality.
Parameters:   'pool_file' = name of the JSON file (must be organized as {key: [values]}).
'key_field' = the OTHER field whose value determines which list to pick from.
Example:      If modality is 'CT', pick from CT descriptions. If 'MR', pick from MR descriptions.
--- sequential_prefix ---
What it does: Generates an auto-incrementing number with a fixed text prefix.
When to use:  When you need a unique ID that always increases (e.g., accession numbers).
Parameters:   'prefix'    = the text that goes before the number (e.g., 'COCSNV').
'zero_pad'  = how many digits total, padded with leading zeros (e.g., 10 -> 0001705200).
Note:         The counter is tracked automatically and persists across restarts.
--- current_time ---
What it does: Sets the field to the exact date/time when the study is created.
When to use:  For timestamps that record 'when did this happen' at creation time.
Parameters:   None needed.
Example:      The 'study_introduced_at' field.
--- lifecycle_timestamp ---
What it does: Leaves the field EMPTY at creation. It gets filled in automatically when the
study reaches a specific lifecycle stage.
When to use:  For timestamps tied to status changes (e.g., 'assigned_at' fills when status = Assigned).
Parameters:   'trigger_status' = the status that triggers this field to be filled.
Example:      'assigned_at' has trigger_status='Assigned'. When study becomes Assigned, the timestamp is set.
--- weighted_random ---
What it does: Picks from a list of values, but some values are MORE LIKELY than others.
When to use:  When you want a realistic distribution (e.g., CT exams are more common than NM).
Parameters:   'possible_values' = list of allowed values.
'weights'         = matching list of probabilities (must add up to 1.0).
Example:      Modality with CT=55%, CR=15%, DX=8%, MR=10%, US=8%, NM=4%.
--- weighted_random_numeric ---
What it does: Picks a random integer from a range, with weights favoring certain sub-ranges.
When to use:  For numeric fields like priority where middle values are more common.
Parameters:   'min', 'max' = the overall range.
'weight_ranges' = sub-ranges with their probabilities.
Example:      Priority 1-10 where 4-6 is most common (50%), 1-3 is 35%, 7-10 is 15%.
--- modality_based_range ---
What it does: Generates a random decimal number, but the range depends on the study's modality.
When to use:  When a numeric value varies by exam type (e.g., RVU differs for CT vs CR).
Parameters:   'ranges' = a dictionary mapping each modality to [min_value, max_value].
Example:      RVU for CT is 1.5-4.0, but for CR it's only 0.2-0.7.
--- initial_value ---
What it does: Always sets the field to the same fixed value when created.
When to use:  For fields with a known starting value (e.g., status always starts as 'Introduced').
Parameters:   'value' = the fixed starting value.
--- self_or_pool ---
What it does: Sometimes copies another field's value ('self'), sometimes picks from a pool.
When to use:  For fields like 'assigned_by' where the radiologist might self-assign (70%)
or a different doctor assigns the study (30%).
Parameters:   'self_field'       = which field to copy from when 'self' is chosen.
'self_probability'  = probability of self-assignment (0.0 to 1.0).
'pool_file'        = fallback pool file for non-self cases.
'pool_key'         = key in that pool file.

fields.name: accession_number
fields.description: Unique identifier for each study. Format: COCSNV + 10-digit zero-padded number.

fields.name: patient_name
fields.description: Random patient name from pool of 1000. Format: Last, First Middle.

fields.name: mrn
fields.description: Unique per patient. Looked up from the patient pool based on patient_name (1:1 mapping).

fields.name: dob
fields.description: Patient date of birth. Looked up from patient pool (1:1 with patient_name).

fields.name: modality
fields.description: Imaging modality. CT is most common, NM is least common.

fields.name: study_description
fields.description: Short description of the exam. Depends on modality (CT has CT descriptions, MR has MR descriptions, etc.).

fields.name: priority
fields.description: Priority level 1-10 (10 = highest). Most studies are mid-range (4-6).

fields.name: rvu
fields.description: Relative Value Unit. Range depends on modality. CT/MR are higher than CR/DX.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Lifecycle status. Always starts as 'Introduced'. Managed automatically by the lifecycle engine.

fields.name: study_introduced_at
fields.description: Timestamp when the study first entered the worklist.

fields.name: assigned_at
fields.description: Timestamp when the study was assigned to a radiologist. Empty until assignment happens.

fields.name: assigned_radiologist
fields.description: Which radiologist is assigned to read this study. Set when status becomes Assigned.

fields.name: assigned_by
fields.description: Who assigned the study. 70% chance the radiologist self-assigned; 30% a referring physician assigned it.
